new in the short term, and I think we're hiring, well, I think we're making an offer to a CM, at least on a contract basis, but she might go, she's either going to Panzer or the Strategy Sprint, so that's not good news for everyone, but it does mean, like, we're looking for good people, and we're trying to backfill.

It also basically adds it to your history so that as it is a site that you've referenced before, the likelihood of them using it, you know, and synthesizing results will be increased.
So there's a lot of good signals from this, and I'll link the article in a minute.
Yeah, so you mentioned, like, having some open questions.
If questions on that and wanting to test it in the thread, what do you think the first steps for that would be?

So it's super easy to add.
So there really isn't any prep.

So, yeah.
I wonder if the, like, if this is the first time they've done it, which would be, what, two days ago?
So Monday would be when they started.
Yeah, I hadn't seen it before.
I don't know if that's Monday or, you know, semi-plus recently, but it's definitely me.
I would wonder, Dave, what your thoughts would be, like, it just opens to, like, read this article.

I wonder if there'd be any value or hypothetical value of, like, putting the title of the article or, like, summarize this article about blah, blah, blah from this company or adding, like, a little bit of extra context on that click just to, like, even cede further to connect brand or title or something.
I don't know.
Yeah, in the prompt, could potentially, because the prompt is part of the click.

So read from Airbyte so I can ask questions about it, right?
So I think there in the prompt, we can probably.
Probably add some additional context to it.
Oh, that's funny.
It's a totally different answer.
This is the first time that I asked it, and this is the second time I asked it.
Interesting.
Yeah.

There's a third question there.
I'm so sorry, I apologize.
Go ahead.

Okay, and Dave, it sounds like you have an article that, like.
Shared, shares this hypothesis of, like, why people would do this?
Yeah.
Did you drop it in the chat?
I'll drop it in the chat in second.
You've to find it.
Yeah.
Okay, and then I wanted to do, like, a kind of highlight of a client who, like, keeps coming back to, like, data and geo questions.
And, like.
Mara, I know, like, you've been going back and forth with Galileo, and I think Jason's, like, been helping advise you on some of that stuff.

Yeah, for sure.
I think, so Jason and I haven't talked about this.
I think they're, so let me answer your questions.
First would be kind of like the general setting.
Connor and Jackson are the two stakeholders who we speak with most of the time.
They attend the meetings.
They're the people that we have the primary relationship with.
We've discussed scrunch with them over the last couple of weeks, and we knew that would be something post-Connor going on PTO.
So we would want to all walk through together.
Jason.
Peace.
Peace.
Tens to, so he'll kind of drop into the chat questions that he has.
Sometimes they're not necessarily like an accurate reading of the dashboards that we have.
So a lot of it is kind of like adding context and then going back and forth on sort of what he's looking at and what trends we're seeing.
So he dropped a question on AI citations and was concerned that maybe we didn't have the correct strategy in place for LLMs.
Mostly because I think he's going to be having a conversation with like, I would imagine the board at some point.
And it's just trying to get ahead of the things that he doesn't understand just yet.
So he asked us about scrunch because he knew that we'd discussed like, we're, we're going to be getting this up and running for you.
Um, and yeah, I think the, the thread that Jason.

Responded to, I think his interpretation was that we had already showed scrunch to Galileo, and that we had mentioned citations versus like mentions as sort of the core metric for AI, which thankfully was not the case.

Um, but this week to, anyway, also I should say I messaged Jason just over the weekend, our Jason, when Jason from Galileo shared a message on like Saturday, just like wanting to go through things in a little bit more depth.
I shared it with Jason Gong in a message just saying like, Hey, I know people, I don't know if I gave him this context, but I know directors have been pulling in Marcel for like an Atlas walkthrough.
I was like, this is just one of our most skeptical, skeptical clients.
If you're available, I could see it being valuable for you to join and we can talk through things.
Otherwise, can I share like a few talking points with you just ensure that I'm capturing everything.
Um, if you have any like concerns or anything, you can let me know.
And I didn't hear back on that.
So I just kind of had the conversation with Jason and Vivek.
And we talked through Scrunch generally, just sort of some of the things that we will be tracking for them with the caveat that like, we're still nailing down the prompts.
Jason had feedback there as well, which was very helpful.
And so anyway, I didn't want to show them prompts that I didn't feel fully confident about.
So I walked them through generally Scrunch.
We talked about our general positioning on Geo.
We had the conversation around like focusing on citations versus mentions.
And then just like knowing Jason's personality, obviously Jason from Galileo, we have to reiterate the same things kind of over and over.
So I figured what might be helpful is also just having like written responses.
I've shared all of the like memos, um, and the output, um, piece that we have on Geo with

And if he feels like he has to kind of justify, or that's not really how he's thinking about it, but I think he said it himself.
He was like, I think I'm just a little paranoid that all of this search is going away and what's the use of producing more content.
And if anything is just, if everything is going to be showing up as like, you know, competitor content, like what's the use in doing any other type of content?

And so we talked a little bit about that, and then I booked a session with him for like three weeks from now, when we'll have the data from Scrunch that like we can actually start drawing insights from just that.
He feels like it's not, you know, vanishing into thin air.
And then I have a meeting with someone from Scrunch, think it's Andrew, immediately after this call, just to go through the like V2 of the prompts.
And based on like Jason's feedback, and then also the feedback that we've chatted through, I don't know if it was this week or last week on prompts.
Basically, all of them are, I nixed like all of the things that were informational, or like more informational, and replaced it with like direct product evaluation.
And so anyway, I think it should be in a better position, but Andrew, we'll chat through it together, and I'll share any learnings that we have.
And then in terms of Galileo, the conversation is ongoing.
I also mentioned to them that I could share the prompts with them, and also share a loom of just like contextualizing why we picked the prompts that we did, just so that they feel like.
Like, they have the visibility.
Like, I don't know that they tend to ask.

Anyway, this will be a, like, test and learn situation where figuring out how much information is useful for, like, buy-in, trust, like, feeling like they're collaborating with us versus what leads us into rabbit holes that, like, aren't necessarily super directly related and difficult to get out of.
So, anyway, I know that was a monologue, but there's, like, an executive summary that we'll be doing for Jason and pulling him in three weeks from now to discuss scrunch.
And then on the day-to-day level, we have a good rapport with, like, Jackson and Connor, and we'll just keep talking about it with them, but both of them are on PTO this week and next week.
So, anyway, I can pause there, but that's kind of the general gist.
No, that's perfect.
I asked you to do a monologue, so thank you for doing a monologue.
Well, I'm glad that that's, yeah, it's a lot.
But also the thing is, something I'll share, too, just so that it's, I think.
Jason's mannerisms or, like, how he communicates in our external channel is, like, it's not, it could be misconstrued almost as, like, antagonistic.
He is very direct, which is great because it gives us an opportunity to respond.
There are challenges where sometimes I don't think that, like, we're on the same page with, like, the data that we're looking at.
Um, but when we're on a call with him, he is much more collaborative and, like, it's much warmer.
Um, so.
Yeah, think Vivek's responses were well done, you know?
I mean, he's, like, providing the quantitative information that, you know, we can counter with or at the very least contextualize with.
So I thought it was good.
Yeah, I mean, direct is good also, you know?
Don't dissimble.
Yeah.
Tell us what you think.
And I responded to him over the weekend, Jason and I had a back and forth of, like, and I essentially just reiterated our position on Geo.

And helped him understand that, like, or I should say, like, I shared a couple of studies from, like, Ahrefs with him where, you know, the one from February of this year and then one from, like, or no, one from June of this year and one from February of this year, just showing, like, the general trajectory of, like, overall site traffic from LLMs.

And then where we're, what we're seeing currently for Galileo, which is very comfortable, but it makes sense that they're just a little, this is going to be a continual conversation, and it makes sense that, like, he's kind of fixated on it, but yeah, that's where we are.

Yeah, and then are, are you thinking about setting, like, any shared, I mean, I know where it's, like, weird stuff to track for them, but are they, are they setting goals, and, like, are they telling you about them, and are we able to help shape those or discourage them?
Yeah, that's a good question.
Nothing like explicit yet.
I mean, we just talked about like some of the prompts that would be most important to be visible in.
And from Jason's perspective, I think it's just focusing on showing up in like competitor content and just making sure that like Galileo is continually part of the conversation.
Um, but other than that, nothing more like specific yet.
So I think maybe also once I have a better handle of like what I can like comfortably promise them, you know, like I can help like guide that conversation.
But yeah, nothing like extremely explicit just yet.
Okay, yeah.
I mean, I think right now, it seems like a lot of our clients are doing H2 planning and setting goals.
So we should just be trying to figure out if they're setting goals that we're supposed to be influencing.
And if he's talking to the executives about like showing up in LLMs, I'm sure somebody has a number somewhere in their brain.
Cool.
Anybody have anything else?
All right.
Thanks, guys.
Thanks.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly Director Standup to discuss ongoing projects, client updates, and strategic initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New CM hiring in progress; Panzer taking over upcoming Strategy Sprint clients](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)
  - [Scrunch dashboards set up for clients; opportunity to refine prompts and data](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [Airbyte's ChatGPT button implementation discussed; potential for testing and implementation](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [Galileo client case study: ongoing discussions about AI/LLM strategy and content visibility](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)

## Topics

### Team Updates and Capacity Management

  - [Panzer taking over new clients from Strategy Sprint (e.g., Metronome, Monograph)](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=346.0)
  - [Potential new CM hire, either for Panzer's team or Strategy Sprint](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Addressing capacity issues for Rachel and Jesselyn:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)
      - [Shadowing their process to identify improvement areas](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)
      - [Shifting manual publishing tasks to Suleman (EPD requests)](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=427.0)
      - [Considering UX study approach to identify patterns and engineering needs](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### Scrunch Dashboard Implementation

  - [Basic dashboards set up for clients; directors now have editor access](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
  - [Opportunity to improve:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=526.0)
      - [Upload more prompts](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Refine ICP and competitor data](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Mara working on V2 prompts for Galileo, focusing on direct product evaluation](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1203.0)

### Airbyte's ChatGPT Button Feature

  - [Dave highlighted Airbyte's "Summarize with ChatGPT" button](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [Potential benefits:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
      - [Forces ChatGPT to review the content](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
      - [Adds to ChatGPT's history, potentially increasing future references](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [Considerations for testing:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=655.0)
      - [Timing of implementation (possibly Monday)](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=709.0)
      - [Potential to add extra context in the prompt (e.g., article title, company name)](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Custom-built by Airbyte, not an OpenAI embed](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)

### Galileo Client Case Study

  - [Key stakeholders: Connor, Jackson, and Jason (CEO)](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=936.0)
  - [Ongoing discussions about AI/LLM strategy and content visibility](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
  - [Addressing Jason's concerns:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [AI citations vs. mentions](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1112.0)
      - [Impact of LLMs on search traffic](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [Justification for continued content production](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1177.0)
  - [Next steps:](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
      - [Scrunch walkthrough and data review in 3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
      - [Preparing written responses and memos for Jason](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)
      - [Balancing information sharing for trust vs. avoiding unnecessary complexities](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1283.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Dave to share article about ChatGPT button implementation benefits](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
  - [Mara to meet with Scrunch representative to refine prompts for Galileo](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1203.0)
  - [Prepare executive summary and Scrunch data review for Galileo in 3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)
  - [Directors to check if clients are setting H2 goals that GrowthX should influence](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=1494.0)
  - [Continue monitoring and addressing team capacity issues](https://fathom.video/share/4zQysQebfLh-XjAV38QBf36nrYv1zUeo?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)
